EEGReference: TP9/TP10 (linked mastoids, applied offline during preprocessing)
TaskDescription: Somatosensory evoked potential, vibration on left hand 50 ms, ISI 2-4 s, 30 trials/run (runs 04 & 11)
InstitutionalDepartmentName: School of Biomedical Engineering, Health Science Center

StimulusPresentation.AdditionalHardware: Arduino Uno for sensory stimuli via serial port; Nokia WH-102 headphones; 3W LED with TES-1332A light meter; 1027 disk vibration motor

SubjectArtefactDescription: Bad channels identified manually and interpolated with the mean of three surrounding channels (22 subjects had bad channels). Eye blink and eye movement artifacts removed by ICA — components identified by visual inspection of scalp topographies, time courses, and spectra (Huang et al., 2020). No bad epoch rejection.
StimulusDescription: 1027 disk vibration motor on left hand, 50 ms duration, 3 W rated power, 80% efficiency, 10 mm x 2.7 mm; delivered via Arduino Uno
Note: This file contains pseudo-continuous EEG reconstructed by concatenating pre-epoched 4-second segments from the M3CV competition dataset. The original continuous BrainVision recordings are not publicly available. Data was preprocessed (filtered, ICA-cleaned, re-referenced, downsampled) before distribution.
Instructions: Remain still. Visual, auditory, and somatosensory stimuli will be presented in random order.
